title: Embracing the Future of Education: Online Learning
passage:
The Rise of Online Learning

Online learning, also known as distance education, has gained immense popularity in recent years. With the advent of technology, students can now access a wide range of courses from the comfort of their homes. This mode of education offers flexibility, allowing learners to study at their own pace and schedule. Many universities and colleges have embraced online platforms, providing students with the opportunity to earn degrees without the need to attend classes in person.

One significant advantage of online learning is the ability to connect with instructors and peers from around the globe. This interaction can enhance the learning experience, as students are exposed to diverse perspectives and ideas. Additionally, online courses often incorporate multimedia resources, such as videos and interactive quizzes, which cater to different learning styles.

However, online learning is not without its challenges. Some students may struggle with self-discipline and motivation, leading to lower completion rates compared to traditional classroom settings. Moreover, the lack of face-to-face interaction can make it difficult to build relationships and network with fellow students.

Despite these challenges, the future of online learning looks promising. As technology continues to advance, we can expect even more innovative approaches to education, making learning accessible to everyone, regardless of their geographical location.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What is one benefit of online learning mentioned in the passage?

options.A: It allows students to study at their own pace.
options.C: It requires students to attend classes in person.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct because the passage specifically states that online learning offers flexibility, allowing learners to study at their own pace.

questions.text: Online learning makes it easier to build relationships with classmates.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the lack of face-to-face interaction can make it difficult to build relationships, indicating that this statement is false.

questions.text: Does the author believe online learning will improve in the future?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the future of online learning looks promising, suggesting the author's positive belief about its improvement.

questions.text: Match the following challenges of online learning with their descriptions.

descriptions.A: Can lead to lower completion rates.

questions.instruction: Provide a specific term or a number.
questions.text: What do online courses often incorporate to cater to different learning styles?

vocabulary.meaning: the ability to adapt to new conditions
the ability to control oneself and make oneself do things
the percentage of students who finish a course or program

tactics:
Hướng dẫn

When approaching the passage, pay attention to the main ideas and specific details. Look for keywords in the questions that can guide you back to the relevant sections in the text. Practice skimming for the main ideas and scanning for specific information to answer the questions effectively.
